behavior_summary: U8P_GRAVEGG::hatch orchestrates an egg-hatching sequence: it freezes the player/avatar, fades the screen to black, moves/teleports the avatar to the egg location, replaces/destroys egg items and creates temporary/new items representing the hatchling, plays sound and visual frame changes, spawns a short-lived effect, fades the screen back, and then returns control to the player.

The script performs two main phases: a pre-fade/post-teleport replacement loop that removes original egg instances and creates intermediate items while setting a transient global flag, and a post-fade animation/finalization loop that finishes item replacement, runs frame-based visual updates, plays SFX, spawns a short-lived process, clears the transient flag, and completes cleanup.

Multiple external engine services are used and awaited (fade processes, teleport, spawn), and the script uses a global flag (magicWallDone) as a transient synchronization/guard during replacement and animation.
notable_control_flow: Start: call setAvatarInStasis(1) to freeze the avatar (prevent player control).
Suspend and wait on a FadeToBlack process, then call a teleport/position helper (teleportToEgg) to place the avatar at the egg.
Iterate over nearby/related items (loop using a local iterator) to destroy egg items and create/pop temporary replacement items; during this loop the script sets global magicWallDone = 1.
Suspend and wait on a FadeFromBlack process after creating initial replacements and before final animation.

Run a second loop that destroys temporary replacements and creates final hatched items, plays SFX (playSFX), updates item frames (Item::setFrame) driven by a small loop counter, and spawns a short-lived effect (spawn FREE::1C19) — waiting for the spawn return as part of the sequence.
Clear magicWallDone (set to 0), increment/compare a local frame/counter ([BP-04h]) against a threshold (0x0E) to drive Item::setFrame calls, then call setAvatarInStasis(0) to unfreeze the avatar.
Suspension points (implied/suspend) are used after each external process call so the visual/audio/teleport steps complete before proceeding.
open_questions: What exact item type IDs and subtype parameters correspond to the created/hatched items (the constants observed such as 02D8h, 0236h, 0Eh were noted but their gameplay meanings are not fully resolved).
What precise selection criteria determine which world items are targeted by the iterator (shape, distance, ownership, or a specific relation to the egg)?
Is magicWallDone used elsewhere in game logic (global restart guard or broader synchronization), or is its use limited to this hatching transition?
Can multiple eggs U8P_GRAVEGG::hatch in a multi-step or parallel fashion, and how does this script behave if invoked concurrently (reentrancy and interaction with magicWallDone)?
What exact visual effect/process is spawned by FREE::1C19 and how long does it last (cosmetic flash, particles, gameplay effect)?

main_function.name: U8P_GRAVEGG::hatch
main_function.purpose: Handle the egg-hatching event by freezing the avatar, performing a fade/teleport to the egg, replacing/destroying egg items and creating hatched item(s) with visual/sound feedback, spawning a short-lived effect, and restoring avatar control.
entry_conditions: Inferred: invoked when an actor/avatar triggers or interacts with the egg (fast-area entry / use activation).

function_map.name: U8P_GRAVEGG::hatch
function_map.role: Event handler implementing the egg-hatching cinematic and item-replacement sequence.

function_map.name: U8P_GRAVEGG::setAvatarInStasis
function_map.role: Engine helper to freeze/unfreeze the avatar during the sequence (called with 1 then 0).

function_map.name: U8P_GRAVEGG::FadeToBlack
function_map.role: Process invoked to fade the screen to black before teleporting/altering items.

function_map.name: U8P_GRAVEGG::teleportToEgg
function_map.role: Process/helper that moves or teleports the avatar to the egg location after fade-out.

function_map.name: Item::destroy
function_map.role: Engine routine to remove item instances (used to delete eggs and temporary items).

function_map.name: Item::create
function_map.role: Engine routine to create item instances used for hatched results or temporary replacements.

function_map.name: Item::pop
function_map.role: Finalize/populate newly created items (place in world / set extra properties).

function_map.name: U8P_GRAVEGG::playSFX
function_map.role: Play U8P_GRAVEGG::hatch sound effect during finalization.

function_map.name: Item::setFrame
function_map.role: Change an item's visual frame to animate U8P_GRAVEGG::hatch progression.

function_map.name: spawn FREE::1C19
function_map.role: Spawn a short-lived free process/effect during the U8P_GRAVEGG::hatch animation.

function_map.name: U8P_GRAVEGG::FadeFromBlack
function_map.role: Process to fade the screen back from black after changes are applied.

external_calls.purpose: Freeze avatar at sequence start and unfreeze at end (1 then 0).
Hide teleport and item replacement by fading screen to black; script waits for completion.
Place avatar/camera at egg location prior to U8P_GRAVEGG::hatch animation (three stack args observed).
Remove original egg item instances and later remove temporary replacement items.
Create temporary and final hatched items with specific type/subtype constants.
Finalize and place created items into the world (called after Item::create).
Reveal the scene after teleport and initial item modifications; script waits for completion.
Play a U8P_GRAVEGG::hatch sound when final items are created/animated.
Animate visual frames of the hatched item(s) using a counter-driven frame value.
Create a short-lived visual/effect process as part of the U8P_GRAVEGG::hatch animation and wait for its completion/return.

writes.name: magicWallDone
writes.likely_effect: Used as a transient synchronization/guard during the hatching transition: set to 1 while initial replacements are created and cleared (set to 0) during/after final animation. Appears to prevent other interactions or indicate transition-in-progress.

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: U8P_GRAVEGG::hatch orchestrates an egg-hatching sequence: it freezes the player/avatar, fades the screen to black, moves/teleports the avatar to the egg location, replaces/destroys egg items and creates temporary/new items representing the hatchling, plays sound and visual frame changes, spawns a short-lived effect, fades the screen back, and then returns control to the player.
